steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a subject-verb agreement question (Standard English Conventions). The sentence structure is “The radiation that [blank] during the decay of radioactive atomic nuclei is known as gamma radiation.” The blank fills the verb in the relative clause “that [blank].” The subject of that clause is “radiation,” which is singular.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘occurs’: Singular present-tense verb; agrees with the singular subject “radiation.” ✓
  • Choice B — ‘have occurred’: Plural verb (“have” requires a plural subject); violates subject-verb agreement with the singular “radiation.” ✗
  • Choice C — ‘occur’: Plural base form; used with plural subjects or with “I”, not with singular third-person “radiation.” Violates subject-verb agreement. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘are occurring’: Plural progressive form (“are” requires a plural subject); violates subject-verb agreement with the singular “radiation.” ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
